{
  "judge_id": "J-123",

  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the contradiction between the opening and closing theses) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., the need for more information about the teacher VLM).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free and identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., the internal contradiction).",
    "RX.5": "The agent does not provide any annotation or source for non-cosmetic changes."
  },
  "confidence": {

    "R1.2": 0.95,

    "RX.3": 1.0,

  "blockers": []

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (the contradiction between the opening and closing theses) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (e.g., the need for more information about the teacher VLM).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free and identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., the internal contradiction).
rationale.RX.5: The agent does not provide any annotation or source for non-cosmetic changes.
